source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 50 US jurisdictions and 100+ countries. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Sequoia, Spark, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



In this role, a Software Engineer you'll design, build, and operate the systems that support our product and the engineers who build it. We're looking for someone who wants to own their impact and find simple solutions to complex problems that are tailored to our needs. We use GCP, Pulumi, Postgres, and TypeScript, but prior experience is not required.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Take responsibility for Anrok's reliability, security, scalability, and performance.

 - Drive technical decisions about our infrastructure.

 - Identify and fix bugs and technical debt.

 - Collaborate with other engineers and management to build high-impact solutions.

 - Example project areas:
   
   - Improve system observability and provide tools to enable product engineers to own application observability.
   
   - Scale our database layer through sharding, replication, and splitting out services when it makes sense.
   
   - Refactor existing systems to take into account present and evolving needs.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You have a strong technical background, with 5+ years of experience in software engineering.

 - You have 5+ years of experience operating cloud-deployed software at scale using config-as-code.

 - Experience managing relational databases at scale is a plus, but not required.

 - You have a security mindset and think carefully about edge cases.

 - You think through problems thoroughly to ensure we're building the right solution to the right problem at the right time, and you will push back when necessary.

 - Your designs take into account current and future needs, technical debt, and maintenance, and you can clearly articulate the tradeoffs.

 - You level up the people around you by sharing your knowledge and experience.

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.



WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Daily lunch and snacks for those working out of our San Francisco office.

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance covered 100%.

 - One Medical membership covered, flexible sick benefits and more.

 - Annual learning and development stipend for books, online courses, and conferences, as well as a curious team to share your learnings with.

 - Annual team offsites and in-person opportunities around our growing Anrok hubs.

 - Home office setup stipend to ensure you have the equipment you need to thrive at work.



At Anrok, we embrace a dynamic and flexible hybrid work environment based out of our growing office hubs - San Francisco, New York City, and Salt Lake City where we collaborate in-person 3 days per week.

For employees

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Platform and AI Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 50 US jurisdictions and 100+ countries. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Sequoia, Spark, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



As the Platform and AI Infrastructure Engineering Manager, you'll own the shared systems and AI infrastructure on which the rest of Anrok builds on. As we grow, well-designed platform spanning authn and authz, RBAC, FGAC, audit trails and more, along with AI infrastructure is the backbone that lets every other team move fast without compromising the compliance workflows our customers depend on.

You have the opportunity to drive execution and strategy for an exceptional team, help shape the product roadmap, and coach and develop senior engineers. We work primarily in TypeScript and React, but prior experience is not required.



 


IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Hire, manage, and grow a talented team building the platform and AI infrastructure that powers Anrok's product.

 - Set the product and technical strategy, while driving processes for high quality execution.

 - Write and review code alongside the engineers on the team as needed.

 - Foster a collaborative and healthy culture of feedback, opportunity, and growth for the team.

 - Contribute to the Anrok engineering culture and initiatives.

 - Find appropriate uses of AI in both the creation of automations or as the automation itself.

 - Help grow the team—mentoring engineers, contributing to technical culture, and raising the bar on how we build.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 3+ years of experience managing engineering teams, with significant experience as a software engineer.

 - Track record of figuring out how to set individuals and teams up for success.

 - You enjoy coding and have strong technical acumen to guide the team through product and technical decisions.

 - Experience building AI infrastructure—you've built and operated the systems beneath LLM-powered or agentic products in production, and you understand firsthand what makes them reliable and maintainable at scale.

 - A track record of building shared services and components that support internal engineering teams—the foundational primitives and tooling that the rest of the org builds on, designed to make every other team faster.

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Daily lunch and snacks for those working out of our office hubs.

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance covered 100%.

 - One Medical membership covered, flexible sick benefits, and more.

 - Annual learning and development stipend for books, online courses, and conferences, as well as a curious team to share your learnings with.

 - Annual team off-sites and in-person opportunities around our growing Anro

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Connectivity
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Sequoia, Spark, Index, and Khosla Ventures.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL

As the Product Manager, you’ll own a critical piece of the customer journey: getting customers' transaction data into Anrok, which is the foundation for our tax automation. In addition, you’ll drive how we leverage this data to solve additional customer needs.

Key projects:

 - Identify the next frontier of product and business opportunities for Anrok

 - Make integrations self-serve for faster customer onboarding

 - Build and scale our integration partner ecosystem to reach more customers

 - Define the strategy for serving customer financial analysis needs with Anrok data
   
   


WHAT EXCITES US

 - Have 6+ years product management experience, 3+ years building B2B products

 - Are excited by bringing clarity to ambiguous problems

 - Enjoy wearing different hats in a lean environment

 - Think like a GM with strong product and business instincts

 - Communicate clearly and concisely, both verbally and in writing

 - Lead with curiosity, understanding, and a growth mindset

 - Work well cross-functionally (eg. sales, support, finance, marketing, leadership)

 - Will do what it takes to make your product and team successful

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.
   
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Daily lunch and snacks for those working out of our office hubs.

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance covered 100%.

 - One Medical membership covered, flexible sick benefits, and more.

 - Annual learning and development stipend for books, online courses, and conferences, as well as a curious team to share your learnings with.

 - Home internet reimbursement stipend.

 - Wellness reimbursement program.

 - Annual team off-sites and in-person opportunities around our growing Anrok hubs.

 - Home office setup stipend to ensure you have the equipment you need to thrive at work.



At Anrok, we embrace a dynamic and flexible hybrid work environment based out of our growing office hubs - San Francisco, New York City, Salt Lake City, and Dublin where we collaborate in-person 3 days per week.



Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Anrok recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with an anrok.com http://anrok.com domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Anrok via other sources should be ignored.

source_jobs.description:

As the Activation Product Manager, you’ll own a critical piece of the customer journey: built and lead our product-led growth motion. Today, thousands of early-stage companies discover Anrok, but our sales-assisted onboarding can’t scale with growing volume. We're leaving growth on the table. Our vision is for Anrok to be in every company's founding stack: you get incorporated in Delaware, buy Rippling, buy Anrok.

We need someone to transform our onboarding experience from sales-assisted to self-serve so companies can get compliant in minutes, not days — and then grow with us into becoming the next generation of enterprise customers.




IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL

 - Make our onboarding process self-serve and the fastest on the market

 - Automate our tax registrations process so customers get compliant quickly

 - Define and scale product-led acquisition channels

 - Identify the next frontier of product and business opportunities for Anrok

source_jobs.title: Field Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We are seeking a Field Marketing Manager to own our field marketing strategy and execution. This role will identify and execute high-impact conferences and events, plan customer and prospect dinners, and drive Sales Qualified Opportunities from field activities.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Develop and execute quarterly field marketing calendar, identifying key conferences, trade shows, and industry events that align with target accounts and revenue goals.

 - Plan and execute executive dinners, hospitality events, and regional roadshows to engage prospects and customers in target markets.

 - Own end-to-end event logistics including vendor management, booth design, collateral, registration, and on-site execution to deliver seamless experiences.

 - Manage swag inventory, budget, and ordering to ensure sales and marketing teams have materials for events and prospect engagement.

 - Train Account Executives on event attendance best practices, pre-event prospecting, booth engagement strategies, and post-event follow-up to maximize event ROI.

 - Own Sales Qualified Opportunity generation metric from field marketing activities, tracking leads from events through to qualified pipeline and closed revenue.

 - Build reporting dashboards to measure event performance including attendance, leads generated, Sales Qualified Opportunities created, pipeline influenced, and ROI by event type.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 4+ years of field marketing or event marketing experience in B2B SaaS with a proven track record generating pipeline.

 - Experience in field marketing, event marketing, or demand generation with focus on conferences and in-person events in B2B SaaS.

 - Proven track record planning and executing events that generate qualified pipeline, with measurable impact on Sales Qualified Opportunities and revenue.

 - Strong project management skills with ability to manage multiple events simultaneously, juggle vendors, timelines, and cross-functional stakeholders.

 - Experience with event marketing tools and CRM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot) to track event performance and attribution from registration through closed deals.

 - Budget management experience including forecasting event costs, tracking spend, and calculating ROI to justify field marketing investments.

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance covered 100%.

 - One Medical membership covered, flexible sick benefits, and m

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer, Pre Sales
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading AI-driven tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $50M from leading investors including Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We're seeking a Solutions Engineer to drive revenue growth by guiding strategic customers through complex technical integrations. You'll be instrumental in moving Anrok upmarket, working at the intersection of sales, product, and engineering to close deals and ensure successful implementations. You'll become the technical expert customers rely on by mastering our API capabilities, understanding complete quote-to-cash workflows, and developing deep expertise in sales tax compliance and third-party billing integrations (Stripe, NetSuite, and more).

Working closely with Sales, Activation, and Product/Engineering teams, you'll bridge the gap between technical possibility and business outcomes. You'll help drive continued rapid growth and establish Anrok as the definitive solution for software sales tax automation.



IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Play an integral role in the standardized Anrok sales process, helping achieve key milestones and exit criteria to effectively move customers through their evaluation process while working closely with Account Executives on deal strategy

 - Become an expert in the various paths to integrating with Anrok between pre-built integration functionality, our APIs, and billing workflows

 - Consult with customers and prospects to design and execute on the best solution for their specific business, serving as the primary technical contact from presales through implementation

 - Act as a trusted advisor on sales tax compliance, Anrok's platform, and API integration best practices for prospects, customers, and partners

 - Work closely with our Onboarding teams to provide effective and efficient technical support through implementation

 - Serve as the voice of the customer by gathering technical feedback and requirements from prospects and customers, then partnering with Product and Engineering to influence roadmap decisions and drive product improvements

 - Break down complex technical challenges and concepts for internal and external teams, to foster shared understanding and a clear path toward resolution

 - Design workflows to effectively prioritize and manage live calls, email responses, documentation of implementation paths and decisions, and other strategic projects

 - Help surface and implement new ideas, such as improving our technical documentation or designing new demo functionality for our third party integrations




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You have 3+ years of experience as a Sales or Solutions Engineer.

 - You thrive in understanding complex problems, crafting inventive solutions, and mapping out a clear path forward and executing on it.

 - You’ve got extensive experience working with REST APIs, webhooks, and integrations

 - You’re as comfortable speaking with business executives and non-technical teams as you are with developers and technical stakeholders.

 - Strong written and verbal communicatio

source_jobs.title: Sales Tax Specialist, Physical Goods
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 50+ US jurisdictions and many countries worldwide. Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We’re looking for a Sales Tax Specialist who cares about working with customers on simplifying complex problems and building and scaling great processes. You will be involved in helping Anrok execute and build key processes around US & international tax compliance.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Drive engagement between Anrok’s Legal, Finance, and Tax (LFT) and Engineering teams to develop further and build out Anrok’s international indirect tax platform. Specifically:
   
   - Research and documentation of taxability determinations, tax rates, and compliance requirements
   
   - Driving the preparation and filing of tax returns for customers
   
   - Engage with third-party accounting partners on internal research projects and engage them in the platform

 - Engage with Anrok customers to help support them through onboarding and simplifying complex tax questions for them

 - Help to support analysis and direction for ad hoc projects related to international and US taxes

What does a typical week look like?

 - Assist with research on US & International tax matters and regulatory changes

 - Analyze tax implications of new business activities and product features

 - Support Product Tax Code research and validation

 - Assist with registration and compliance research

 - Respond to tax technical questions from internal teams

 - Support customer-facing teams with tax guidance

 - Assist with customer onboarding and activation questions

 - Contribute to knowledge base development




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You have a strong background with 2-5 years of experience in indirect tax (nice to have if international VAT experience), including researching technical issues, as well as the registration and returns processes

 - You have hands-on experience with physical goods indirect tax compliance, not just digital or software-specific tax regimes

 - You enjoy engaging with customers and walking them through tax technical processes in a clear and thoughtful manner

 - You have some exposure to other indirect tax regimes and compliance processes

 - You love solving problems and always push for change and improvement

 - You have an entrepreneurial attitude and passion to build a business from the ground up

 - Strong written and verbal skills paired with a solutions-oriented approach to customers

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Daily lunch and snacks for those working out of our San Francisco office.

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance covered 100%.

 - One Medical m

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $50M from leading investors including Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We're seeking a Post-Sales Solutions Engineer to help drive expansion within our existing customer base. You'll transform technical expertise into measurable revenue outcomes, working at the intersection of sales, product, and engineering to unlock new opportunities and ensure long-term partnerships.

You'll become the trusted technical advisor customers rely on, mastering our platform capabilities and developing deep expertise in sales tax compliance and enterprise integrations. By bridging customer business objectives with technical solutions, you'll establish Anrok as the strategic partner customers rely on as they scale their business.



IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Own technical discovery for expansion — conduct ongoing discovery within existing accounts to uncover new use cases, geographic expansion, and integration opportunities. Create technical roadmaps that map customer objectives to Anrok capabilities.

 - Partner on account strategy — work closely with Account Managers on expansion prioritization, account planning, and QBRs. Provide technical qualification and strategic insights that inform forecasting.

 - Lead complex implementations — drive expansion implementations including multi-entity configurations and advanced feature deployments. Create parallel workstreams to accelerate time-to-value and monitor implementation health proactively.

 - Run live technical consultations — lead troubleshooting calls and configuration sessions with confidence. Demonstrate composure during high-pressure escalations and adapt technical depth based on your audience in real-time.

 - Be the Voice of the Customer — channel technical feedback, integration requirements, and product gaps to Product and Engineering to influence roadmap priorities.

 - Manage technical escalations — own the Technical Solutions queue for complex configurations and troubleshooting. Maintain SLAs for response and resolution, document issues comprehensively, and escalate to Engineering with complete context.

 - Build the playbook — create implementation guides, expansion frameworks, and reusable assets. Mentor new team members and deliver internal training as the SE function scales.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 5+ years as a Solutions Engineer or Solutions Architect in SaaS

 - Deep experience with REST APIs, webhooks, SQL, and enterprise integrations — especially in billing, financial, or compliance software

 - You default to automation — you use AI and modern tooling to move faster, document better, and scale your impact beyond what's possible through manual effort alone

 - You stay calm under pressure — comfortable leading escalation calls and adapting your approach in real-time

 - You distill complex technical concepts for any audience, from engineering teams to C-suite executives

 - You're a fast learner who builds genuine domain expertise and proactively closes knowled

We are looking for a Go To Market (GTM) Engineer focusing on AI Adoption, who can code, build, and deploy intelligent and agentic systems that transform how our revenue team operates. You’ll sit at the intersection of software engineering and go-to-market strategy, building systems and automation workflows, deploying services on GCP, and integrating Claude into production pipelines that run reliably at scale. This is a hands-on technical role where you'll own the full lifecycle from writing code to deploying securely within our existing infrastructure—shifting our GTM motion from human-capacity-first to AI-first, directly impacting deal velocity, team productivity, and our ability to grow 3–4x without proportionally growing headcount.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Design and execute the AI adoption strategy for the GTM team—identifying high-impact use cases for Claude, building reusable prompt libraries and AI agents, and establishing quality and security standards for LLM usage.

 - Write and maintain production code (Python, JavaScript) that powers GTM automation workflows—from API integrations and data pipelines to Claude-powered agents that handle lead qualification, call analysis, and proposal generation, and more.

 - Define and manage the deployment process for GTM-owned applications within a dedicated GCP project—including CI/CD pipelines, monitoring, logging, and security best practices for AI-powered services.

 - Design and implement secure integrations between systems, such as Salesforce, Gong, Zendesk, GCP, and the Claude API—building robust data pipelines with proper authentication, error handling, and retry logic.

 - Architect end-to-end automation workflows that eliminate manual work across the sales cycle—including signal-based lead enrichment and routing, post-call processing, pipeline management, and RFP response generation—building targeting infrastructure that compounds as the team scales.

 - Create internal documentation, runbooks, and training content to drive team adoption; conduct hands-on sessions that help teammates incorporate AI tools into their daily workflows.

 - Stay current on AI capabilities, GCP services, and industry best practices; recommend new tools and approaches that keep Anrok at the frontier of GTM productivity.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 4 - 7+ years in a technical GTM, revenue operations, sales engineering, or software engineering role with hands-on experience building and deploying production systems.

 - Strong coding skills in Python and/or JavaScript—you write clean, maintainable code, are comfortable owning services in production, and have fluency with APIs, webhooks, and integration patterns.

 - Cloud infrastructure experience (GCP Preferred)—you’ve deployed and managed services using Cloud Functions, Cloud Run, BigQuery, or Pub/Sub, and you understand IAM, service accounts, and

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



Anrok is growing quickly, and as an Account Manager, you’ll play a key role in scaling our expansion motion. You will manage a book of existing Anrok sellers and consult with them to support managing their sales global tax risk as their revenue scales. You’ll work closely with our Head of Activation and senior leadership team to refine the growth sales motion and develop new initiatives to strengthen the seller experience.   




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Manage a book of Anrok’s sellers to drive further platform adoption.

 - Manage all strategic contract renewals. 

 - Consult with Anrok sellers on their international sales tax compliance strategies. 

 - Collaborate with finance, customer solutions, and operations teams to ensure a seamless customer billing, expansion, and renewal experience. 




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 2-3 years of experience in a SaaS closing and/or account management role

 - Experience collaborating with customer success and onboarding teams 

 - Experience with a technical sale, selling into Finance and Engineering teams

 - A track record of high performance against sales goals 

 - Strong interpersonal and communication skills 

 - Self-starters who are organized and resourceful

 - Experience with Gong, Outreach, and Salesforce

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Daily lunch and snacks for those working out of our San Francisco office.

 - Medical, dental, and vision insurance covered 100%.

 - One Medical membership covered, flexible sick benefits and more.

 - Annual learning and development stipend for books, online courses, and conferences, as well as a curious team to share your learnings with.

 - Annual team offsites and in-person opportunities around our growing Anrok hubs

 - Home office setup stipend to ensure you have the equipment you need to thrive at work.



At Anrok, we embrace a dynamic and flexible hybrid work environment based out of our growing office hubs - San Francisco, New York City, and Salt Lake City where we collaborate in-person 3 days per week.



Please be aware that Anrok does not recruit via WhatsApp or any third-party messaging platforms. All official communication from our team comes exclusively from @Anrok.com http://Anrok.com email addresses. If you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from Anrok, please do not engage. All open roles are listed on this page

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Agentic AI Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 50 US jurisdictions and 100+ countries. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Sequoia, Spark, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We're building the agentic infrastructure layer at Anrok—the systems that let AI agents reason about tax data, take action across jurisdictions, and operate reliably at scale. This is early and foundational work: you'll be designing and shipping the primitives that the rest of the product builds on. We're looking for an engineer who pairs a strong distributed-systems background with real, hands-on experience building AI agent systems—someone who understands both how to make a service fault-tolerant and what it takes to make an LLM-powered agent reliable in production. If you want to work on distributed systems that sit at the intersection of AI and fintech, this role is for you.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Design and build the distributed, fault-tolerant infrastructure that powers Anrok's AI agent capabilities—covering memory, state management, and execution for autonomous systems.

 - Own technical architecture decisions for new agent infrastructure services end-to-end, from initial design through production deployment and iteration.

 - Build the evaluation, observability, and reliability tooling that lets the rest of the team ship agents confidently on top of your infrastructure.

 - Collaborate closely with product engineers and our tax data team to translate real compliance workflows into reliable, scalable agent primitives.

 - Drive engineering best practices across the team: code quality, testing discipline, system design, and operational health.

 - Identify and fix performance, reliability, and scalability bottlenecks as our agent systems grow.

 - Help grow the team—mentoring engineers, contributing to technical culture, and raising the bar on how we build.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You have a strong systems engineering background, with 3+ years of experience building and operating distributed, production-grade systems.

 - You've designed services that are scalable, resilient, and fault-tolerant—and you've done it more than once.

 - You've built and deployed AI agents or LLM-powered systems in production, and you understand firsthand what makes them reliable and maintainable at scale.

 - You're familiar with the modern AI stack—prompt engineering, eval frameworks, RAG pipelines, tool calling, and agent orchestration—and you have a point of view on what the infrastructure beneath it should look like.

 - You think carefully about architecture and tradeoffs, and you're comfortable leading technical decisions without a complete playbook.

 - You have experience with the full software development lifecycle: design, code review, testing, deployment, and on-call ownership.

 - You're energized by ambiguity—you can take a fuzzy problem, break it down, and ship something real.

 - You're curious and motivated to learn new tools—you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built some

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.

Anrok is hiring a Product Marketing Manager to bring our products to market and arm our sales team to win. You will own a set of features and product areas end to end, from positioning and launch to the enablement that helps sellers tell the story.

This role sits at the center of product and sales. You will work shoulder to shoulder with product managers to shape what we ship and how we frame it, and with sales to turn that framing into pipeline and closed revenue.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Own end-to-end go-to-market for a set of Anrok products and features, from positioning and launch through adoption and revenue impact, working across product, sales, and marketing.

 - Develop and evolve the positioning, messaging, and value propositions that set Anrok apart, making complex tax infrastructure clear, credible, and hard to ignore for buyer personas across the commercial, mid-market, and enterprise segments.

 - Maintain the messaging frameworks and positioning documents that serve as the single source of truth, so the whole organization stays consistent across every channel, campaign, and customer touchpoint.

 - Own a segment of sales enablement: build the assets sellers actually use (decks, one-pagers, battle cards, demo scripts, objection-handling guides) and the training that makes them stick, all grounded in how buyers really evaluate, so sellers handle objections, beat competitors, and move deals.

 - Turn customer proof, wins, and references into evidence that sharpens messaging and fuels the funnel.

 - Serve as the internal voice of the market, translating customer feedback, competitive analysis, and market trends into clear recommendations, and partnering with product managers to shape roadmap priorities.

 - Define and track the metrics that prove product marketing's impact, including pipeline influenced, win rates, sales asset utilization, feature adoption, and time-to-first-value, and use them to guide strategy.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 4+ years of product marketing experience ideally in SaaS, fintech, or infrastructure with proven track record of driving measurable GTM success and revenue impact in fast-paced, high-growth environments

 - Expertise in product positioning and messaging frameworks, with demonstrated ability to translate technical capabilities into compelling customer value for B2B audiences, particularly in complex or technical product categories

 - Strong sales enablement experience with concrete examples of creating assets that sales teams actively use and that demonstrably improve win rates, deal velocity, or average deal size

 - Project management and cross-functional leadership skills, with ability to drive initiatives from strategy through execution without extensive direction or hand-holding

 - Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with portfolio of messaging frameworks, positioning

source_jobs.title: Strategic Finance Manager
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 50+ US jurisdictions and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We're expanding our Strategic Finance team and looking for a Strategic Finance Manager to help strengthen and scale the revenue finance function. This is a high-ownership seat for someone who wants to influence how a fast-growing SaaS company invests across its full revenue engine: sales, partnerships, marketing, and post-sale expansion. Reporting to the Head of Finance, you'll be the primary thought partner on all things revenue — partnering with leaders across sales, marketing, partnerships, and activation to translate financial insight into operating decisions.

This is one of the rare strategic finance seats where your customer base is your peer group — Anrok's product is bought by Finance teams at the fastest-growing software companies in the world. You'll think like an operator and learn from the operators on the other side of the table. A great fit for someone with strong analytical foundations, comfortable with the pace and ambiguity of an early-stage company, and energized by scaling a function rather than maintaining one.



IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own financial planning and forecasting across the full revenue org — new business, expansion, partnerships, and marketing

 - Bring financial perspective into territory design, quota capacity, marketing investment, partnership economics, and post-sale expansion strategy — working alongside the Head of Finance to influence GTM decisions

 - Own CAC payback as the primary GTM efficiency metric across segments, channels, and motions; surface what's working and what isn't

 - Model contribution margin and gross margin by customer segment, product line, and acquisition channel

 - Maintain the ARR waterfall — new logo, expansion, contraction, churn — and reconcile to billing actuals

 - Support board materials, investor diligence prep, and scenario modeling for strategic decisions

 - Run ad hoc analyses spanning competitive benchmarking, pricing sensitivity, market expansion economics, and capital allocation tradeoffs

 - Build AI workflows for recurring revenue finance analysis using the team's AI-native tooling stack (Omni, Salesforce, Python, n8n, Claude) — reducing manual cycles and unlocking deeper analytical work

 - This role is currently an IC role and structured to grow with the function — as Anrok scales, so does the scope and ownership of this seat




WHAT EXCITES US:

 - 4+ years in investment banking, private equity, or venture investing — you can build a model from scratch, stress-test assumptions, and know what "good" looks like across business stages

 - 2+ years in a strategic finance or FP&A role supporting GTM functions — sales, marketing, partnerships, or post-sale

 - Deep fluency in B2B SaaS revenue metrics: pipeline coverage, SQO creation, CAC payback, NRR, magic number, contribution margin, and channel economics

 - Demonstrated interest or experience

As our Accounting & Advisory Partner Manager, you'll source, build, and scale strategic partnerships with accounting firms, CPA practices, and financial advisory organizations that serve our target customers. You'll develop joint go-to-market strategies that create new revenue streams, identify co-sell and referral opportunities, and enable partner teams to confidently recommend Anrok to their clients. Your partnerships will be a key growth lever for both Anrok and our partner ecosystem.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:


SOURCE & DEVELOP NET NEW PARTNERSHIPS

 - Identify and recruit high-value accounting and advisory partners, regional CPA firms, boutique advisory practices, and outsourced CFO providers — whose client bases align with Anrok's growth strategy

 - Build principal and director-level relationships with partner leadership teams to unlock strategic initiatives


ENABLE PARTNER TEAMS FOR SUCCESS

 - Deliver enablement programs that equip partner accountants and advisors to effectively identify, position, and refer Anrok to their clients

 - Collaborate with Anrok's sales and customer success teams to leverage partner relationships in active deals

 - Guide development of co-marketing materials, referral playbooks, and client-facing positioning for accounting and advisory audiences


SHAPE PARTNER STRATEGY

 - Work cross-functionally with product, marketing, and sales leadership to inform accounting and advisory partnership priorities and roadmap

 - Lead partner presence at industry events and conferences — including accounting, tax, and CFO-focused events — to build pipeline and strengthen relationships

 - Track partnership performance metrics and continuously optimize strategies for maximum impact




WHAT EXCITES US:

 - 3+ years in accounting firm partnerships, channel sales, or business development within B2B SaaS, with a proven track record of meeting or exceeding revenue targets

 - Demonstrated success sourcing and closing net

source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We’re making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



We’re looking for an experienced technology leader to own and evolve Anrok’s internal technology infrastructure as we scale. As our first Head of Security & Business AI Applications, you’ll be the single owner of corporate IT, application security, compliance obligations, and AI-powered internal tooling—building the systems that let a rapidly growing team operate securely and efficiently. This is a high-impact role reporting to our COO, with direct influence over how Anrok runs as a company.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Build the infrastructure to support teams building AI-powered internal tools and infrastructure, deploying LLM-based automations, internal copilots, and agentic workflows to improve how Anrok operates at scale

 - Lead Anrok’s application security program, partnering with engineering to identify risks, drive secure development practices, and protect sensitive customer financial data

 - Own corporate IT infrastructure and operations—including endpoint management, SaaS stack administration, and IT support—managing and developing our IT team member

 - Own all compliance obligations end-to-end, including SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and GDPR—serving as the DRI for audits, renewals, and cross-functional readiness

 - Develop and enforce security policies, access controls, and governance frameworks that grow with the business

 - Partner with Legal and Finance on data privacy obligations, vendor risk management, and third-party security assessments

 - Evaluate, procure, and manage the internal technology stack, ensuring we’re using the right tools as we scale

 - Report on security and compliance posture to executive leadership as needed




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 10+ years in IT, security, or a related technical role, with 3+ years in a leadership position at a scaling tech company

 - Hands-on experience building or deploying AI/LLM-powered internal tools or automations—you default to AI-first solutions for operational problems

 - Strong application security fundamentals—you can review AppSec findings, work with engineers on remediations, and evaluate risk without needing to write the code yourself

 - Genuine familiarity with GDPR and international data privacy obligations, relevant to a fintech handling customer financial data across jurisdictions

 - Track record of building lightweight but durable compliance and governance programs—practical, not checkbox theater

 - Proven ownership of SOC 2 Type II renewals end-to-end—you’ve been the DRI, not just a contributor

 - Technically credible with strong business fluency: you can present to a board, negotiate a vendor contract, and review a penetration test in the same week

 - Self-starter who thrives in ambiguity, builds scalable systems, and knows when to automate vs. hire

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on y

source_jobs.title: VAT Manager (APAC/Australia)
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.



Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Spark Capital, Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Advise on GST registration requirements, rate determinations, and compliance obligations for businesses operating in Australia and other APAC regions.

 - Be the subject matter expert for the APAC regions.

 - Train our tax operations team to oversee VAT compliance operations across APAC markets, ensuring accurate filings for Anrok and our customers.

 - Partner with product teams to shape how we handle specific tax challenges, including e-invoicing mandates, fiscal representation requirements, and country-specific filing obligations.

 - Support customer implementations and escalations involving APAC global tax compliance.

 - Stay ahead of regulatory changes across and translate them into actionable guidance for the business.
   
   


WHAT EXCITES US

 - 3+ years of experience in GST/VAT compliance

 - Current registration with the Australian Tax Practitioners Board (TPB) as a Tax Agent or BAS Agent, with authority to lodge BAS returns on behalf of clients.

 - Relevant Australian tertiary qualification: a degree or postgraduate award in accounting from an Australian institution, or Certificate IV in Financial Services (Bookkeeping or Accounting) from a registered training organisation, plus completion of TPB-approved courses in Australian taxation law and commercial law

 - Strong working knowledge of VAT registration, filing, and reporting requirements

 - Based in Dublin with the right to work in Ireland.

 - You’re curious and motivated to learn new tools — you've experimented with AI in your work or on your own, you're excited about what's possible, and you've built something with it (an application, a workflow, a creative solution to a real problem) that you can walk us through.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Annual learning and development stipend for books, online courses, conferences, and a curious team to share your learnings with.

 - Team offsites and gatherings throughout the year.



At Anrok, we embrace a dynamic and flexible hybrid work environment based out of our growing office hubs - San Francisco, New York City, Salt Lake City, and Dublin where we collaborate in-person 3 days per week.



Please be aware, job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by malicious actors looking for personal data. Anrok recruiters will only reach out via LinkedIn or email with an anrok.com http://anrok.com domain. Any outreach claiming to be from Anrok via other sources should be ignored.

source_jobs.title: Content Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
Anrok is the leading tax automation platform enabling businesses to expand globally without compliance complexity.

As the digital economy has grown 6x over the last decade, software businesses have gone from not worrying about sales tax to needing to monitor exposure, calculate rates, and file returns across 20+ US states and many countries worldwide. This creates a critical bottleneck for companies that should be able to transact with customers everywhere.

Anrok eliminates this complexity by connecting with billing and payment systems to automate tax monitoring, calculations, and filing end-to-end. Our unified platform handles the ever-changing maze of tax laws at municipal, state, and federal levels—so companies can focus on growth, not compliance.

 

Our customers include:

 - 40% of Forbes Top 50 AI companies

 - 20% of Forbes Top 100 Cloud companies

 - Top companies like Notion, Anthropic, and Cursor

We're making compliant digital commerce a reality for companies big and small, backed by over $100M from leading investors including Sequoia, Index, and Khosla Ventures.



Anrok is hiring a Content Marketing Lead to own how the company shows up in the market through editorial and digital content. You will set the content roadmap, direct the creative, and build the body of work that makes finance and operations leaders stop scrolling.

This is a builder's seat. You will own the strategy and ship the work, partnering closely with marketing and sales and managing our brand and creative agencies. The job is to take a sharp point of view on tax infrastructure and turn it into content people actually want to read, watch, and share.

Tax is not where most people expect to find great content — that is exactly the opening. Anrok has a real point of view, customers building the future, and a category that is wide open. You'll define how a tax infrastructure company earns the market's attention.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL

 - Own the content roadmap end to end, from strategy and social/editorial calendar to the work that ships, mapped to brand and pipeline goals.

 - Set creative direction across formats: long-form editorial, thought leadership, video, social, newsletters, and campaign assets.

 - Own the external content voice and build an editorial point of view on tax, finance, and infrastructure that could only come from Anrok.

 - Manage agency and freelance partners across writing, design, and video. Brief the work, hold the bar, and keep the voice consistent.

 - Partner with product marketing and sales to turn launches and customer stories into content that moves deals.

 - Create for every stage of the funnel, from category narrative at the top to proof at the bottom.

 - Own distribution. Decide where content lives, how it travels across channels, and how it compounds into an audience over time.

 - Stay ahead of how content gets found and consumed, including AI-driven search and the feeds your audience actually reads.

 - Tie content to engagement, audience growth, and pipeline, and move budget toward what performs.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - 5+ years creating content for a B2B audience, ideally in SaaS, fintech, or infrastructure.

 - A portfolio with range: digital first, sharp writing, and a few wins that broke through.

 - Strong editorial judgment and a clear view of what great looks like.

 - Experience directing creative and managing agencies or freelancers, not only producing the work yourself.

 - Fluency in how modern audiences discover and consume content, from search to social to AI answers.

 - A bias for shipping. You publish, learn, and iterate rather than wait for perfect.

 - The ability to make a complex category land with a finance or operations leader in a single read.

 - The experience of building a content engine or editorial brand from an early stage.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - The equity upside of an early-stage startup with the product-market fit of a later-stage company.

 - Daily lunch and snacks for

notes: High volume of engineering and product roles, with a focus on tax automation and AI.
